instruction:
Create a single-page Node-Watch dashboard. Build in /root/sovereign/shared/results/node-watch-dashboard/:

1. index.html - Complete dashboard page that:
   - Uses Tailwind CSS via CDN for styling
   - Uses Chart.js via CDN for metrics visualization
   - Works without build step (vanilla JS only)
   - Shows all servers in a responsive grid with status indicators (green/yellow/red)
   - Auto-refreshes every 30 seconds
   - Each server card shows: hostname, status, last seen, current CPU/RAM/disk percentages
   - Click on server card opens detailed view with charts for last 24h
   - API calls to http://localhost:9081/api/v1/

2. Design requirements:
   - Clean, professional interface
   - Status indicators: green (healthy), yellow (warning), red (critical)
   - Real-time updates with loading indicators
   - Responsive design (mobile-friendly)
   - Error handling for API failures
   - No authentication required (read-only dashboard)

3. Dashboard features:
   - Server overview grid
   - Real-time status updates
   - Detailed metrics charts (CPU, Memory, Disk, Load)
   - Process list display
   - Network usage tracking
   - Alert history (future enhancement placeholder)

Use modern JS (fetch API, async/await). Include proper error handling and loading states. Production-ready code that works immediately when the API is running."
